{"results": [{"task_index": 0, "status": "completed", "summary": "STATUS: REQUEST_CHANGES
SUMMARY: PRD scope and high-level rollout are directionally sound, and most core flows are defined well enough to outline an implementation sequence, but it is not yet planning-ready for execution without avoidable churn. The main gaps are missing explicit Phase 2 API/route-to-page dependency mapping, incomplete validation coverage for several required write/error paths, and unresolved inconsistencies between “design freeze” and the remaining open questions. In addition, branch hygiene is called out as mandatory context but not elevated to an explicit pre-implementation gate, which is risky given the noted uncommitted Phase 2 work.

PHASES:
- Phase 0 — Preflight and execution gate: (1) make branch hygiene/Phase 2 commit-boundary cleanup an explicit entry criterion, (2) enumerate the exact existing Phase 2 APIs/helpers each page and form will call, (3) freeze unresolved v1 UI decisions so template structure is not reworked mid-implementation.
- Phase 1 — Auth/session foundation: (1) implement `/web/login` GET/POST with bootstrap CSRF, (2) establish server-side web session model and authenticated CSRF for subsequent POST forms, (3) enforce unauthenticated redirects for dashboard/queue/profile/detail routes, (4) verify PRG behavior and explicit login failure feedback.
- Phase 2 — Read-only operator surfaces: (1) build shared server-rendered layout/navigation/flash framework, (2) implement dashboard from existing summary APIs only, (3) implement blocked/failed/stale queue pages with the required columns and filter semantics, (4) implement profile registry/workload read view.
- Phase 3 — Task detail aggregation view: (1) implement task detail page with metadata, runs, events, messages, and scratchpad sections, (2) define and render empty states for each section, (3) handle stale-after-open/read-refresh behavior when tasks change underneath the operator.
- Phase 4 — Mutations and feedback loop: (1) add profile `is_active` and `routing_metadata` update forms, (2) add reassign/unblock/retry/archive actions on queue/detail pages, (3) standardize POST-redirect-GET success/error flash handling, (4) verify conflict/inactive/auth/session-expiry failures are surfaced explicitly.
- Phase 5 — Browser validation and release hardening: (1) add browser smoke coverage for login → dashboard → queue → detail → action, (2) cover blocked/failed/stale paths plus console-error assertions, (3) verify no-JS/minimal-JS operability, (4) confirm displayed counters/workload values match backend API responses exactly.

DEPENDENCIES:
- Branch hygiene and Phase 2 commit cleanup must be completed before any Phase 3 implementation branch begins.
- Page implementation depends on an explicit page-to-API contract map; otherwise frontend/server helpers may accidentally recreate aggregation logic forbidden by the PRD.
- Authenticated form actions depend on the session model and authenticated CSRF scheme being defined before queue/profile/detail mutations are built.
- Queue filters depend on a documented mapping of “assignee/profile filter” semantics to current backend contract behavior.
- Task detail rendering depends on confirming whether the backend already provides a stable aggregated detail contract or whether the server must orchestrate multiple existing endpoints without adding new business logic.
- Browser tests depend on deterministic seed data/fixtures for blocked, failed, stale, archived, inactive-profile, and conflict scenarios.

RISKS:
- Drift risk: without a per-page API dependency table, implementers may re-aggregate counters/workload/detail data in web handlers; mitigation: require an explicit contract matrix in the PRD/design.
- Validation gap risk: several required behaviors are not acceptance-tested today; mitigation: expand acceptance criteria to cover every in-scope mutation and failure mode.
- UX rework risk: open questions remain despite a stated design freeze; mitigation: resolve or explicitly defer those items before template work starts.
- State-concurrency risk: task/profile state can change between page load and form submit; mitigation: define expected stale/conflict UX and test 409/session-expiry flows.
- Operational risk: uncommitted Phase 2 changes can contaminate implementation scope and reviewability; mitigation: add a hard preflight gate for branch and commit boundary cleanup.

BLOCKING_FINDINGS:
- The PRD does not explicitly map each v1 page/action to the existing Phase 2 API contracts/helpers it must reuse, which makes dependency sequencing ambiguous and creates a high risk of violating the “no second aggregation layer” rule.
- Acceptance criteria do not fully cover required write/error paths from the functional requirements and failure modes, including authenticated CSRF on non-login forms, session expiry during POST, inactive-assignee/auth-failure handling for reassign, invalid `routing_metadata` JSON handling, and the no-JS/minimal-JS requirement.
- Section 14 still lists v1-shaping open questions even though the document says the design is frozen; this inconsistency should be resolved or those questions must be explicitly marked out-of-scope/post-v1.
- The noted Phase 2 uncommitted-work/branch-hygiene prerequisite is only advisory in rollout notes, but the linked requirement/design context says it is a blocker; it should be promoted to an explicit go/no-go precondition in the PRD.", "api_calls": 3, "duration_seconds": 56.13, "model": "gpt-5.4", "exit_reason": "completed", "tokens": {"input": 68999, "output": 2513}, "tool_trace": [{"tool": "skill_view", "args_bytes": 20, "result_bytes": 2001, "status": "ok"}, {"tool": "skill_view", "args_bytes": 18, "result_bytes": 2101, "status": "ok"}]}, {"task_index": 1, "status": "completed", "summary": "STATUS: REQUEST_CHANGES
SUMMARY: The proposed server-rendered, API-reusing operator console is the right architectural direction for hiveX v1 and fits the stated constraints, but the PRD is still missing a few system-level contracts that are necessary to implement it safely and without backend/UI drift: authenticated web-session semantics, composite task-detail degradation behavior, and write-path semantics for profile/task mutations.

KEY_FINDINGS:
- The overall shape is correct: embedding the console into the existing FastAPI service, using server-rendered HTML with POST-redirect-GET, and forbidding a second aggregation layer is a strong fit for the current platform maturity and operational goals.
- Interface boundaries are still under-specified for the most complex page: task detail requires metadata, runs, events, messages, and scratchpad, but the PRD does not define whether this is a single backend-facing helper/composite contract or multiple page-time fetches, nor how partial failure/timeouts should render without breaking operator usability.
- The admin session model is incomplete at architecture level: the PRD mentions login/session/CSRF, but not cookie properties, session rotation, idle/absolute expiry, logout/invalidation, or behavior when the upstream admin credential/backend auth becomes invalid mid-session.
- Mutation semantics are not crisp enough for safe implementation: `routing_metadata` editing, reassign/retry/archive/unblock refresh behavior, and stale-read/conflict handling need explicit request/response/error contracts so the web layer can stay thin without inventing policy.
- Queue filtering is present in acceptance criteria but not in functional scope/requirements detail; the mapping of “profile filter” to “current assignee filter” is noted, but the PRD should state the canonical filter inputs, URL shape, and whether filtering is entirely delegated to existing APIs or adapted server-side.

TRADEOFFS:
- server-rendered HTML + PRG / low JS, simpler ops, easier CSRF/testability, matches existing FastAPI footprint / lower interactivity and more page reloads, more care needed for composite page latency / alternative: SPA with dedicated API client, rejected appropriately for v1
- strict reuse of Phase 2 APIs / avoids duplicate business logic and contract drift, keeps backend as source of truth / may expose gaps where current APIs are not page-shaped enough, especially task detail and filtered queues / alternative: add thin backend-for-frontend helper endpoints that only compose existing control-plane contracts without re-deriving business rules
- admin-only web console reusing admin authority / fastest path to usable operations UI, no RBAC expansion / higher blast radius for any session/auth flaw and stricter need for hardened session lifecycle / alternative: scoped operator tokens or role separation, deferred reasonably but should be named as future hardening

BLOCKING_FINDINGS:
- The PRD does not define the authoritative session/auth contract for the web console (cookie flags, rotation, expiry, logout, mid-session upstream auth failure behavior). For an admin-only control surface, this is a blocker.
- The PRD does not define how the task detail page behaves when one or more required data sources (runs/events/messages/scratchpad) fail, timeout, or return inconsistent versions. Without explicit degradation rules, implementation will either invent policy or produce brittle operator UX.
- The PRD does not define safe write semantics for profile/task mutations, especially `routing_metadata` update behavior (replace vs merge, validation failure rendering, concurrent update/conflict handling). This is necessary to avoid destructive or inconsistent control-plane writes.", "api_calls": 2, "duration_seconds": 41.08, "model": "gpt-5.4", "exit_reason": "completed", "tokens": {"input": 45478, "output": 1361}, "tool_trace": [{"tool": "skill_view", "args_bytes": 20, "result_bytes": 2001, "status": "ok"}]}], "total_duration_seconds": 57.5}
评审结果是 **REQUEST_CHANGES**，所以我不装通过；我现在直接按两位 reviewer 的阻塞项补 PRD，然后再重跑 gate，直到拿到正式结论～ (•̀ᴗ•́)و

+- `GET /web/profiles`：必须复用既有 profile registry / workload contract；禁止自行拼装第二套 workload 聚合。

 ## 9. Acceptance Criteria
 - Given 正确 admin credentials 且 login form 带合法 bootstrap CSRF token, when 提交 Web login form, then 建立会话并以 `302/303` 跳转到 dashboard。
 - Given login form 缺少或携带无效 bootstrap CSRF token, when 提交登录, then 请求被拒绝并返回显式失败反馈。
+- Given 已登录 operator 且 authenticated form 缺少或携带无效 session-bound CSRF token, when 提交 profile/task action form, then 请求在 mutation 之前被拒绝，且页面显示显式失败反馈。
 - Given 已登录 operator, when 打开 dashboard, then 能看到 blocked/failed/stale/profile summary，响应为成功状态，且页面中不存在服务端异常占位内容。
 - Given dashboard 上的 blocked summary, when 点击进入, then 打开 blocked tasks 页面并展示对应任务列表。
 - Given 已登录 operator, when 打开 profile 页面, then 能看到 profile registry 与 workload 信息。
 - Given 某 profile 当前 `is_active=true`, when operator 在 UI 中禁用它, then 页面反馈成功且刷新后该 profile 的 `is_active` 显示为 `false`。
+- Given operator 提交非法 `routing_metadata`（非 JSON object）, when 提交 profile patch form, then 页面显示显式错误，且 profile 状态不发生部分更新。
 - Given 一个 blocked task, when operator 在 queue 或 detail 页面执行 `unblock`, then 页面反馈成功，刷新后该 task 状态为 `ready` 或按既有 contract 进入可重新执行状态。
 - Given 一个 failed 或 stale task, when operator 在 queue 或 detail 页面执行 `retry`, then 页面反馈成功，刷新后该 task 状态为 `ready`，且后续 claim 将创建新的 run。
 - Given 一个可归档 task, when operator 在 queue 或 detail 页面执行 `archive`, then 页面反馈成功，刷新后该 task 状态为 `archived`，并且不再出现在 blocked/failed/stale queue 中。
 - Given 一个 task detail 页面, when 页面加载, then 能看到 task metadata、runs/events、messages、scratchpad 的可读展示（为空时也需有明确 empty-state）。
+- Given task detail 依赖的某个 inspection surface 失败或超时, when 页面加载, then 其他已成功的数据仍可读展示，失败 section 显示明确 degraded/error state，而不是整页崩溃或伪装完整成功。
 - Given reassign 在提交窗口内发生冲突, when operator 提交表单, then 页面显示显式错误，而不是假装成功。
+- Given reassign 的目标 assignee inactive 或当前会话失效, when operator 提交 reassign form, then 页面显示显式失败原因，且 task assignment 不发生部分更新。
 - Given 未登录用户, when 访问 dashboard、queue、profile 或 task detail 页面, then 一律被 `302/303` 重定向到 `/web/login`。
+- Given 已登录会话已过 idle timeout 或 absolute lifetime, when operator 访问任意 `/web/*` 页面或提交 POST form, then 会话被视为失效、服务端不执行 mutation，并重定向回 `/web/login` 或给出等价显式失效反馈。
 - Given 浏览器级 smoke test, when 执行核心流（login → dashboard → queue → detail → action）, then 流程可完成，且浏览器 console 中不存在 uncaught exception 或 error 级别日志。
 - Given queue page filter, when operator 按 assignee/profile 过滤任务, then v1 统一映射到当前 assignee filter 语义，并展示对应任务列表。
+- Given 浏览器禁用 JavaScript 或仅有极少量增强脚本, when 执行 login → dashboard → queue → detail → action 核心流, then 基本操作仍可通过 HTML form + POST-redirect-GET 完成。
@@
 ## 12. Rollout & Migration Notes
 - Phase 3 在现有 FastAPI 服务内联扩展，不新增独立前端服务。
+- **Preflight go/no-go gate**：在任何 Phase 3 编码开始前，必须先创建独立 Phase 3 feature branch/worktree，并确保此前 Phase 2 residue 已被隔离到清晰提交边界；未满足时不得进入实现。
 - rollout 顺序建议：
-  1. 登录/session 基础设施
-  2. dashboard + queue 页面
-  3. task detail 页面
-  4. operator action forms
-  5. 浏览器级验证
-- 当前工作区仍含未提交的 Phase 2 变更；在实现 Phase 3 前必须先整理 branch 与提交边界。
+  1. preflight：branch/worktree hygiene + artifact freeze
+  2. 登录/session/CSRF 基础设施
+  3. dashboard + queue 页面
+  4. profiles 页面与 profile patch forms
+  5. task detail 页面（含 degraded section handling）
+  6. operator action forms
+  7. server-side verification
+  8. 浏览器级验证
@@
-## 14. Open Questions
-- task detail 页面默认信息布局应以 runs/events 为主，还是 messages/scratchpad 为主。
-- v1 dashboard 是否显示 recent exceptions 列表，还是只保留 summary cards + queue links。
-
-> Design freeze: v1 已在 technical design 中冻结为 server-rendered HTML + pure POST-redirect-GET；`routing_metadata` 采用 JSON textarea。
+## 14. Deferred After v1 Freeze
+- task detail 页面默认信息布局在 v1 中不作为 blocker；只要求信息可读、section 明确，视觉优先级优化留到后续迭代。
+- dashboard 的 recent exceptions 列表不进入 v1 必交范围；v1 冻结为 summary cards + queue links 即可。
+
+> Design freeze: v1 已冻结为 server-rendered HTML + pure POST-redirect-GET；`routing_metadata` 采用 JSON textarea；queue filter 统一映射到现有 `assignee_id` contract；`routing_metadata` 写入语义为 replace whole object；Web session 采用服务端 session store + bootstrap/login CSRF 与 authenticated session CSRF 双层防护。
*** End Patch"}

 ### In

 - Web login/admin session
 - Overview dashboard
 - Exception queue pages（blocked / failed / stale）
@@ -64,37 +65,60 @@
 - Complex charting/analytics
 - Artifact explorer
 
+## 7.5. Reuse Contract / Page-to-API Mapping
+- `GET /web/login`：只负责渲染登录页，并 mint bootstrap CSRF token；不做业务聚合。
+- `POST /web/login`：复用现有 admin auth 语义建立 Web session；登录成功后跳转 dashboard。
+- `GET /web/`：必须复用既有 control overview summary contract；禁止自行跨表重算 counters。
+- `GET /web/queues/{queue}`：必须复用既有 exception queue contract；过滤统一映射为现有 `assignee_id` 语义，分页沿用既有 `limit/offset` contract。
+- `GET /web/profiles`：必须复用既有 profile registry / workload contract；禁止自行拼装第二套 workload 聚合。
+- `POST /web/profiles/{profile_id}`：必须复用既有 profile patch contract；`routing_metadata` 由 Web 层做表单解析与 JSON 校验，但最终写入语义仍以既有 backend contract 为准。
+- `GET /web/tasks/{task_id}`：必须复用既有 task-scoped inspection surfaces，展示 task metadata、runs、events、messages、scratchpad；禁止新增绕过 task access path 的读取捷径。
+- `POST /web/tasks/{task_id}/reassign|unblock|retry|archive`：必须复用既有 mutation contract 与状态机；Web 层只做表单绑定、session/CSRF 校验、错误反馈与 POST-redirect-GET。
+- 若现有 API 需要页面级组合，允许新增 thin adapter / backend-for-frontend helper，但其职责只能是**编排既有 contract**，不得重写业务规则、权限规则或聚合真相。
+
 ## 8. Functional Requirements
 1. 系统必须提供一个 Web 登录入口，允许 human operator 使用 admin credentials 建立会话。
 2. 登录成功后，系统必须跳转到 dashboard，而不是停留在原始 JSON 页面。
-3. dashboard 必须展示至少以下 summary：profile 总数、active profile 数、blocked task 数、failed task 数、stale task 数。
-4. dashboard 上的异常 summary 必须可点击进入对应 queue 页面。
-5. queue 页面必须支持按 queue 类型展示任务列表，并至少显示：task_id、title、status、assignee_id、priority、updated_at、is_stale（如适用）。
-6. UI 必须提供 profile 页面，展示 profile registry 与 workload 聚合信息。
-7. UI 必须允许 operator 修改 profile `is_active` 与 `routing_metadata`，并在成功/失败时给出显式反馈。
-8. UI 必须提供 task detail 页面，显示：task 元数据、run history、event log、thread messages、scratchpad entries。
-9. UI 必须提供 reassign 表单，允许 operator 为 task 选择新 assignee，并显示失败原因（如 409 conflict / inactive assignee / auth failure）。
-10. UI 必须提供 unblock / retry / archive 操作入口，并在提交后刷新对应页面状态。
-11. 所有页面必须继续通过 Phase 2 既有 APIs 获取 summary/task-management 数据；禁止 UI 自己从底层原始数据重新计算 dashboard counters。
-12. 所有关键操作失败必须显式显示错误信息；不允许按钮点击后静默无响应。
-13. UI 页面必须保留 API-first 边界：server 端只做会话/模板/表单编排，不重写业务规则。
-14. 首轮实现必须支持无 JavaScript 或最少 JavaScript 的基本操作闭环，确保低复杂度与可测试性。
+3. 系统必须使用服务端持有的 Web session；浏览器 cookie 只保存 opaque session identifier，不得把 admin JWT、密码或可还原认证上下文暴露给浏览器端脚本、URL 或日志。
+4. Web session 必须定义并实现安全默认：`HttpOnly`、`SameSite=Lax`、`Path=/`，在 HTTPS 环境启用 `Secure`；必须具备 idle timeout、absolute lifetime、logout invalidation 与 re-login session rotation 行为。
+5. `POST /web/login` 必须使用 bootstrap CSRF；所有其他有副作用的 `POST /web/*` 都必须使用 authenticated session-bound CSRF；缺失或无效 token 时必须在 mutation 之前拒绝请求。
+6. dashboard 必须展示至少以下 summary：profile 总数、active profile 数、blocked task 数、failed task 数、stale task 数。
+7. dashboard 上的异常 summary 必须可点击进入对应 queue 页面。
+8. queue 页面必须支持按 queue 类型展示任务列表，并至少显示：task_id、title、status、assignee_id、priority、updated_at、is_stale（如适用）。
+9. queue 页面过滤输入在 v1 中必须明确冻结为现有 `assignee_id` 语义；如 UI 文案出现 profile/assignee，服务端映射也必须落到同一既有 backend filter contract。
+10. UI 必须提供 profile 页面，展示 profile registry 与 workload 聚合信息。
+11. UI 必须允许 operator 修改 profile `is_active` 与 `routing_metadata`，并在成功/失败时给出显式反馈。
+12. `routing_metadata` 的 v1 写入语义必须冻结为 **replace whole object**，而不是 merge patch；提交前必须做 JSON object 校验，校验失败时不得产生部分更新。
+13. UI 必须提供 task detail 页面，显示：task 元数据、run history、event log、thread messages、scratchpad entries。
+14. task detail 所依赖的任一 inspection surface 若超时、失败或返回不一致状态，页面必须提供显式降级/错误展示；不得整页静默空白，也不得伪装为完整成功。
+15. UI 必须提供 reassign 表单，允许 operator 为 task 选择新 assignee，并显示失败原因（如 409 conflict / inactive assignee / auth failure）。
+16. UI 必须提供 unblock / retry / archive 操作入口，并在提交后刷新对应页面状态。
+17. 所有页面必须继续通过 Phase 2 既有 APIs 获取 summary/task-management 数据；禁止 UI 自己从底层原始数据重新计算 dashboard counters。
+18. 所有关键操作失败必须显式显示错误信息；不允许按钮点击后静默无响应。
+19. UI 页面必须保留 API-first 边界：server 端只做会话/模板/表单编排，不重写业务规则。
+20. 首轮实现必须支持无 JavaScript 或最少 JavaScript 的基本操作闭环，确保低复杂度与可测试性。
 
 ## 9. Acceptance Criteria
 - Given 正确 admin credentials 且 login form 带合法 bootstrap CSRF token, when 提交 Web login form, then 建立会话并以 `302/303` 跳转到 dashboard。
 - Given login form 缺少或携带无效 bootstrap CSRF token, when 提交登录, then 请求被拒绝并返回显式失败反馈。
+- Given 已登录 operator 且 authenticated form 缺少或携带无效 session-bound CSRF token, when 提交 profile/task action form, then 请求在 mutation 之前被拒绝，且页面显示显式失败反馈。
 - Given 已登录 operator, when 打开 dashboard, then 能看到 blocked/failed/stale/profile summary，响应为成功状态，且页面中不存在服务端异常占位内容。
 - Given dashboard 上的 blocked summary, when 点击进入, then 打开 blocked tasks 页面并展示对应任务列表。
 - Given 已登录 operator, when 打开 profile 页面, then 能看到 profile registry 与 workload 信息。
 - Given 某 profile 当前 `is_active=true`, when operator 在 UI 中禁用它, then 页面反馈成功且刷新后该 profile 的 `is_active` 显示为 `false`。
+- Given operator 提交非法 `routing_metadata`（非 JSON object）, when 提交 profile patch form, then 页面显示显式错误，且 profile 状态不发生部分更新。
 - Given 一个 blocked task, when operator 在 queue 或 detail 页面执行 `unblock`, then 页面反馈成功，刷新后该 task 状态为 `ready` 或按既有 contract 进入可重新执行状态。
 - Given 一个 failed 或 stale task, when operator 在 queue 或 detail 页面执行 `retry`, then 页面反馈成功，刷新后该 task 状态为 `ready`，且后续 claim 将创建新的 run。
 - Given 一个可归档 task, when operator 在 queue 或 detail 页面执行 `archive`, then 页面反馈成功，刷新后该 task 状态为 `archived`，并且不再出现在 blocked/failed/stale queue 中。
 - Given 一个 task detail 页面, when 页面加载, then 能看到 task metadata、runs/events、messages、scratchpad 的可读展示（为空时也需有明确 empty-state）。
+- Given task detail 依赖的某个 inspection surface 失败或超时, when 页面加载, then 其他已成功的数据仍可读展示，失败 section 显示明确 degraded/error state，而不是整页崩溃或伪装完整成功。
 - Given reassign 在提交窗口内发生冲突, when operator 提交表单, then 页面显示显式错误，而不是假装成功。
+- Given reassign 的目标 assignee inactive 或当前会话失效, when operator 提交 reassign form, then 页面显示显式失败原因，且 task assignment 不发生部分更新。
 - Given 未登录用户, when 访问 dashboard、queue、profile 或 task detail 页面, then 一律被 `302/303` 重定向到 `/web/login`。
+- Given 已登录会话已过 idle timeout 或 absolute lifetime, when operator 访问任意 `/web/*` 页面或提交 POST form, then 会话被视为失效、服务端不执行 mutation，并重定向回 `/web/login` 或给出等价显式失效反馈。
 - Given 浏览器级 smoke test, when 执行核心流（login → dashboard → queue → detail → action）, then 流程可完成，且浏览器 console 中不存在 uncaught exception 或 error 级别日志。
 - Given queue page filter, when operator 按 assignee/profile 过滤任务, then v1 统一映射到当前 assignee filter 语义，并展示对应任务列表。
+- Given 浏览器禁用 JavaScript 或仅有极少量增强脚本, when 执行 login → dashboard → queue → detail → action 核心流, then 基本操作仍可通过 HTML form + POST-redirect-GET 完成。
 
 ## 10. Edge Cases / Failure Modes
 - 登录失败

STATUS: PASS | REQUEST_CHANGES | NOT_RUN | BLOCKED
SUMMARY: ...

Review this revised PRD for planning readiness:

# hiveX Human Web Operator Console PRD

## 1. Background
hiveX 已完成两个关键阶段：
- Phase 1：durable orchestration kernel（task/thread/run/event/scratchpad）
- Phase 2：CEO / Operator control plane（overview、exception queues、profile workload、reassign、profile control-plane write）

这意味着 human operator 已经可以通过 HTTP API / MCP 直接管理系统，但实际使用门槛仍高：需要知道接口、手动组装请求、理解底层 contract。产品目前的缺口不再是“有没有 control plane”，而是 **有没有一个真正可用的人类操作界面**。

因此需要启动 **Phase 3 — Human Web Operator Console**，把已完成的 control-plane contract 变成浏览器中可直接使用的管理工作台。

## 2. Problem Statement
当前 hiveX 在“可管理”与“可日常使用”之间仍有断层：
- human operator 需要直接调用 API/MCP，使用成本高；
- overview / exception queue / workload 虽然存在，但缺少统一导航与页面化操作；
- task detail 的 messages / scratchpad / runs / events 只能通过底层接口拼接查看；
- 关键管理动作（reassign / unblock / retry / archive / profile patch）缺少低摩擦 UI 入口；
- 若 Phase 3 不复用既有 API contract，后续会出现 UI 与 control-plane backend 漂移。

## 3. Goals
- 提供 human operator 可直接使用的 Web console。
- 让 operator 能通过浏览器完成 overview → queue → detail → action 的完整管理闭环。
- 复用 Phase 2 control-plane APIs，避免前端重复推导业务聚合逻辑。
- 保持 admin-only v1 权限模型，先交付高效可用的操作面。
- 为后续更丰富的管理体验保留扩展空间，但首轮不引入重前端复杂度。

## 4. Non-Goals
- 不在本轮重做 control-plane backend。
- 不在本轮引入独立 operator token / RBAC 子系统。
- 不在本轮实现 worker-facing Web workspace。
- 不在本轮实现 SPA 微前端、前后端分离部署、Node-based build pipeline（除非设计评审推翻）。
- 不实现 scheduler/watchdog/replanner 自动控制策略。
- 不实现 artifact first-class model。

## 5. Users / Personas
- **Human operator / founder / admin**：登录系统、查看总览、定位异常、分派/重分派任务、查看 task 细节、介入恢复。
- **CEO / coordinator agent（间接受益者）**：受益于 human operator 与自己共享同一业务真相源，但 Phase 3 首轮不为 agent 提供新的浏览器交互面。

## 6. User Stories
- 作为 human operator，我希望登录后第一眼看到 overview dashboard，这样我能快速知道系统是否健康。
- 作为 human operator，我希望从 dashboard 一键进入 blocked / failed / stale 队列，这样我能快速定位异常。
- 作为 human operator，我希望打开 task detail 时，同时看到 task metadata、runs/events、messages、scratchpad，这样我能理解上下文后再决定如何干预。
- 作为 human operator，我希望在页面上直接执行 reassign / unblock / retry / archive，这样我不需要手工构造 API 请求。
- 作为 human operator，我希望看到 profile registry 与 workload，并能启停 profile 或修改 routing metadata，这样我能管理 agent 可用性。
- 作为维护者，我希望 UI 只复用既有 API，而不重复写业务聚合逻辑，这样后续变更只需要维护一套 contract。

## 7. Scope (In/Out)
### In
- Phase 3 preflight gate（独立 feature branch/worktree + 干净提交边界）
- Web login/admin session
- Overview dashboard
- Exception queue pages（blocked / failed / stale）
- Profile registry / workload page
- Task detail page
- Task management actions in UI
- Profile control-plane write in UI
- Minimal navigation / layout / flash error/success feedback
- Browser-level tests for key flows

### Out
- Worker portal
- Rich real-time websockets / live push
- Multi-tenant/org-level permission model
- Standalone frontend app/deployment
- Complex charting/analytics
- Artifact explorer

## 7.5. Reuse Contract / Page-to-API Mapping
- `GET /web/login`：只负责渲染登录页，并 mint bootstrap CSRF token；不做业务聚合。
- `POST /web/login`：复用现有 admin auth 语义建立 Web session；登录成功后跳转 dashboard。
- `GET /web/`：必须复用既有 control overview summary contract；禁止自行跨表重算 counters。
- `GET /web/queues/{queue}`：必须复用既有 exception queue contract；过滤统一映射为现有 `assignee_id` 语义，分页沿用既有 `limit/offset` contract。
- `GET /web/profiles`：必须复用既有 profile registry / workload contract；禁止自行拼装第二套 workload 聚合。
- `POST /web/profiles/{profile_id}`：必须复用既有 profile patch contract；`routing_metadata` 由 Web 层做表单解析与 JSON 校验，但最终写入语义仍以既有 backend contract 为准。
- `GET /web/tasks/{task_id}`：必须复用既有 task-scoped inspection surfaces，展示 task metadata、runs、events、messages、scratchpad；禁止新增绕过 task access path 的读取捷径。
- `POST /web/tasks/{task_id}/reassign|unblock|retry|archive`：必须复用既有 mutation contract 与状态机；Web 层只做表单绑定、session/CSRF 校验、错误反馈与 POST-redirect-GET。
- 若现有 API 需要页面级组合，允许新增 thin adapter / backend-for-frontend helper，但其职责只能是编排既有 contract，不得重写业务规则、权限规则或聚合真相。

## 8. Functional Requirements
1. 系统必须提供一个 Web 登录入口，允许 human operator 使用 admin credentials 建立会话。
2. 登录成功后，系统必须跳转到 dashboard，而不是停留在原始 JSON 页面。
3. 系统必须使用服务端持有的 Web session；浏览器 cookie 只保存 opaque session identifier，不得把 admin JWT、密码或可还原认证上下文暴露给浏览器端脚本、URL 或日志。
4. Web session 必须定义并实现安全默认：`HttpOnly`、`SameSite=Lax`、`Path=/`，在 HTTPS 环境启用 `Secure`；必须具备 idle timeout、absolute lifetime、logout invalidation 与 re-login session rotation 行为。
5. `POST /web/login` 必须使用 bootstrap CSRF；所有其他有副作用的 `POST /web/*` 都必须使用 authenticated session-bound CSRF；缺失或无效 token 时必须在 mutation 之前拒绝请求。
6. dashboard 必须展示至少以下 summary：profile 总数、active profile 数、blocked task 数、failed task 数、stale task 数。
7. dashboard 上的异常 summary 必须可点击进入对应 queue 页面。
8. queue 页面必须支持按 queue 类型展示任务列表，并至少显示：task_id、title、status、assignee_id、priority、updated_at、is_stale（如适用）。
9. queue 页面过滤输入在 v1 中必须明确冻结为现有 `assignee_id` 语义；如 UI 文案出现 profile/assignee，服务端映射也必须落到同一既有 backend filter contract。
10. UI 必须提供 profile 页面，展示 profile registry 与 workload 聚合信息。
11. UI 必须允许 operator 修改 profile `is_active` 与 `routing_metadata`，并在成功/失败时给出显式反馈。
12. `routing_metadata` 的 v1 写入语义必须冻结为 replace whole object，而不是 merge patch；提交前必须做 JSON object 校验，校验失败时不得产生部分更新。
13. UI 必须提供 task detail 页面，显示：task 元数据、run history、event log、thread messages、scratchpad entries。
14. task detail 所依赖的任一 inspection surface 若超时、失败或返回不一致状态，页面必须提供显式降级/错误展示；不得整页静默空白，也不得伪装为完整成功。
15. UI 必须提供 reassign 表单，允许 operator 为 task 选择新 assignee，并显示失败原因（如 409 conflict / inactive assignee / auth failure）。
16. UI 必须提供 unblock / retry / archive 操作入口，并在提交后刷新对应页面状态。
17. 所有页面必须继续通过 Phase 2 既有 APIs 获取 summary/task-management 数据；禁止 UI 自己从底层原始数据重新计算 dashboard counters。
18. 所有关键操作失败必须显式显示错误信息；不允许按钮点击后静默无响应。
19. UI 页面必须保留 API-first 边界：server 端只做会话/模板/表单编排，不重写业务规则。
20. 首轮实现必须支持无 JavaScript 或最少 JavaScript 的基本操作闭环，确保低复杂度与可测试性。

## 9. Acceptance Criteria
- Given 正确 admin credentials 且 login form 带合法 bootstrap CSRF token, when 提交 Web login form, then 建立会话并以 `302/303` 跳转到 dashboard。
- Given login form 缺少或携带无效 bootstrap CSRF token, when 提交登录, then 请求被拒绝并返回显式失败反馈。
- Given 已登录 operator 且 authenticated form 缺少或携带无效 session-bound CSRF token, when 提交 profile/task action form, then 请求在 mutation 之前被拒绝，且页面显示显式失败反馈。
- Given 已登录 operator, when 打开 dashboard, then 能看到 blocked/failed/stale/profile summary，响应为成功状态，且页面中不存在服务端异常占位内容。
- Given dashboard 上的 blocked summary, when 点击进入, then 打开 blocked tasks 页面并展示对应任务列表。
- Given 已登录 operator, when 打开 profile 页面, then 能看到 profile registry 与 workload 信息。
- Given 某 profile 当前 `is_active=true`, when operator 在 UI 中禁用它, then 页面反馈成功且刷新后该 profile 的 `is_active` 显示为 `false`。
- Given operator 提交非法 `routing_metadata`（非 JSON object）, when 提交 profile patch form, then 页面显示显式错误，且 profile 状态不发生部分更新。
- Given 一个 blocked task, when operator 在 queue 或 detail 页面执行 `unblock`, then 页面反馈成功，刷新后该 task 状态为 `ready` 或按既有 contract 进入可重新执行状态。
- Given 一个 failed 或 stale task, when operator 在 queue 或 detail 页面执行 `retry`, then 页面反馈成功，刷新后该 task 状态为 `ready`，且后续 claim 将创建新的 run。
- Given 一个可归档 task, when operator 在 queue 或 detail 页面执行 `archive`, then 页面反馈成功，刷新后该 task 状态为 `archived`，并且不再出现在 blocked/failed/stale queue 中。
- Given 一个 task detail 页面, when 页面加载, then 能看到 task metadata、runs/events、messages、scratchpad 的可读展示（为空时也需有明确 empty-state）。
- Given task detail 依赖的某个 inspection surface 失败或超时, when 页面加载, then 其他已成功的数据仍可读展示，失败 section 显示明确 degraded/error state，而不是整页崩溃或伪装完整成功。
- Given reassign 在提交窗口内发生冲突, when operator 提交表单, then 页面显示显式错误，而不是假装成功。
- Given reassign 的目标 assignee inactive 或当前会话失效, when operator 提交 reassign form, then 页面显示显式失败原因，且 task assignment 不发生部分更新。
- Given 未登录用户, when 访问 dashboard、queue、profile 或 task detail 页面, then 一律被 `302/303` 重定向到 `/web/login`。
- Given 已登录会话已过 idle timeout 或 absolute lifetime, when operator 访问任意 `/web/*` 页面或提交 POST form, then 会话被视为失效、服务端不执行 mutation，并重定向回 `/web/login` 或给出等价显式失效反馈。
- Given 浏览器级 smoke test, when 执行核心流（login → dashboard → queue → detail → action）, then 流程可完成，且浏览器 console 中不存在 uncaught exception 或 error 级别日志。
- Given queue page filter, when operator 按 assignee/profile 过滤任务, then v1 统一映射到当前 assignee filter 语义，并展示对应任务列表。
- Given 浏览器禁用 JavaScript 或仅有极少量增强脚本, when 执行 login → dashboard → queue → detail → action 核心流, then 基本操作仍可通过 HTML form + POST-redirect-GET 完成。

## 10. Edge Cases / Failure Modes
- 登录失败
- session 过期
- API 409 conflict（reassign/retry 等）
- inactive assignee
- task 在打开 detail 后被其他 actor 修改
- scratchpad/messages 为空
- queue 无任务
- routing_metadata 不是合法 JSON（若采用 JSON 编辑）

## 11. Security / Privacy / Permission Notes
- Web session 不得把 admin JWT 直接暴露给浏览器端脚本、URL 或日志。
- login form 与已认证表单都必须具备服务端校验的 CSRF 防护；不得仅依赖 SameSite。
- 所有服务端调用 backend API 时必须沿用 admin 身份，并保持最小泄露面。
- UI 只暴露 admin 通过 API 已可访问的数据，不新增越权入口。
- 错误页/flash message 不得泄露 token、密码或内部堆栈中的敏感信息。

## 12. Rollout & Migration Notes
- Phase 3 在现有 FastAPI 服务内联扩展，不新增独立前端服务。
- Preflight go/no-go gate：在任何 Phase 3 编码开始前，必须先创建独立 Phase 3 feature branch/worktree，并确保此前 Phase 2 residue 已被隔离到清晰提交边界；未满足时不得进入实现。
- rollout 顺序建议：
  1. preflight：branch/worktree hygiene + artifact freeze
  2. 登录/session/CSRF 基础设施
  3. dashboard + queue 页面
  4. profiles 页面与 profile patch forms
  5. task detail 页面（含 degraded section handling）
  6. operator action forms
  7. server-side verification
  8. 浏览器级验证

## 13. Metrics / Success Signals
- 在验证环境中，operator 可通过 Web UI 完成 login → dashboard → queue → detail → action 的完整闭环，无需手写 API 请求。
- blocked / failed / stale 三类异常路径都至少有一条浏览器级 smoke test 覆盖并通过。
- UI 页面展示的 summary/workload 数值与对应 control-plane API 响应一致，不出现第二套聚合逻辑。
- 关键页面（login、dashboard、queue、detail）浏览器验证通过，且无 uncaught exception / error 级别 console log。

## 14. Deferred After v1 Freeze
- task detail 页面默认信息布局在 v1 中不作为 blocker；只要求信息可读、section 明确，视觉优先级优化留到后续迭代。
- dashboard 的 recent exceptions 列表不进入 v1 必交范围；v1 冻结为 summary cards + queue links 即可。

Design freeze summary:
server-rendered HTML + pure POST-redirect-GET; routing_metadata uses JSON textarea; queue filter maps to assignee_id contract; routing_metadata write semantics are replace-whole-object; web session uses server-side session store + bootstrap/login CSRF and authenticated session CSRF.

# hiveX Human Web Operator Console PRD

## 1. Background
hiveX 已完成两个关键阶段：
- Phase 1：durable orchestration kernel（task/thread/run/event/scratchpad）
- Phase 2：CEO / Operator control plane（overview、exception queues、profile workload、reassign、profile control-plane write）

这意味着 human operator 已经可以通过 HTTP API / MCP 直接管理系统，但实际使用门槛仍高：需要知道接口、手动组装请求、理解底层 contract。产品目前的缺口不再是“有没有 control plane”，而是 **有没有一个真正可用的人类操作界面**。

因此需要启动 **Phase 3 — Human Web Operator Console**，把已完成的 control-plane contract 变成浏览器中可直接使用的管理工作台。

## 2. Problem Statement
当前 hiveX 在“可管理”与“可日常使用”之间仍有断层：
- human operator 需要直接调用 API/MCP，使用成本高；
- overview / exception queue / workload 虽然存在，但缺少统一导航与页面化操作；
- task detail 的 messages / scratchpad / runs / events 只能通过底层接口拼接查看；
- 关键管理动作（reassign / unblock / retry / archive / profile patch）缺少低摩擦 UI 入口；
- 若 Phase 3 不复用既有 API contract，后续会出现 UI 与 control-plane backend 漂移。

## 3. Goals
- 提供 human operator 可直接使用的 Web console。
- 让 operator 能通过浏览器完成 overview → queue → detail → action 的完整管理闭环。
- 复用 Phase 2 control-plane APIs，避免前端重复推导业务聚合逻辑。
- 保持 admin-only v1 权限模型，先交付高效可用的操作面。
- 为后续更丰富的管理体验保留扩展空间，但首轮不引入重前端复杂度。

## 4. Non-Goals
- 不在本轮重做 control-plane backend。
- 不在本轮引入独立 operator token / RBAC 子系统。
- 不在本轮实现 worker-facing Web workspace。
- 不在本轮实现 SPA 微前端、前后端分离部署、Node-based build pipeline（除非设计评审推翻）。
- 不实现 scheduler/watchdog/replanner 自动控制策略。
- 不实现 artifact first-class model。

## 5. Users / Personas
- **Human operator / founder / admin**：登录系统、查看总览、定位异常、分派/重分派任务、查看 task 细节、介入恢复。
- **CEO / coordinator agent（间接受益者）**：受益于 human operator 与自己共享同一业务真相源，但 Phase 3 首轮不为 agent 提供新的浏览器交互面。

## 6. User Stories
- 作为 human operator，我希望登录后第一眼看到 overview dashboard，这样我能快速知道系统是否健康。
- 作为 human operator，我希望从 dashboard 一键进入 blocked / failed / stale 队列，这样我能快速定位异常。
- 作为 human operator，我希望打开 task detail 时，同时看到 task metadata、runs/events、messages、scratchpad，这样我能理解上下文后再决定如何干预。
- 作为 human operator，我希望在页面上直接执行 reassign / unblock / retry / archive，这样我不需要手工构造 API 请求。
- 作为 human operator，我希望看到 profile registry 与 workload，并能启停 profile 或修改 routing metadata，这样我能管理 agent 可用性。
- 作为维护者，我希望 UI 只复用既有 API，而不重复写业务聚合逻辑，这样后续变更只需要维护一套 contract。

## 7. Scope (In/Out)
### In
- Phase 3 preflight gate（独立 feature branch/worktree + 干净提交边界）
- Web login/admin session
- Overview dashboard
- Exception queue pages（blocked / failed / stale）
- Profile registry / workload page
- Task detail page
- Task management actions in UI
- Profile control-plane write in UI
- Minimal navigation / layout / flash error/success feedback
- Browser-level tests for key flows

### Out
- Worker portal
- Rich real-time websockets / live push
- Multi-tenant/org-level permission model
- Standalone frontend app/deployment
- Complex charting/analytics
- Artifact explorer

- Given 一个 blocked task, when operator 在 queue 或 detail 页面执行 `unblock`, then 页面反馈成功，刷新后该 task 状态为 `ready` 或按既有 contract 进入可重新执行状态。
- Given 一个 failed 或 stale task, when operator 在 queue 或 detail 页面执行 `retry`, then 页面反馈成功，刷新后该 task 状态为 `ready`，且后续 claim 将创建新的 run。
- Given 一个可归档 task, when operator 在 queue 或 detail 页面执行 `archive`, then 页面反馈成功，刷新后该 task 状态为 `archived`，并且不再出现在 blocked/failed/stale queue 中。
- Given 一个 task detail 页面, when 页面加载, then 能看到 task metadata、runs/events、messages、scratchpad 的可读展示（为空时也需有明确 empty-state）。
- Given task detail 依赖的某个 inspection surface 失败或超时, when 页面加载, then 其他已成功的数据仍可读展示，失败 section 显示明确 degraded/error state，而不是整页崩溃或伪装完整成功。
- Given reassign 在提交窗口内发生冲突, when operator 提交表单, then 页面显示显式错误，而不是假装成功。
- Given reassign 的目标 assignee inactive 或当前会话失效, when operator 提交 reassign form, then 页面显示显式失败原因，且 task assignment 不发生部分更新。
- Given 未登录用户, when 访问 dashboard、queue、profile 或 task detail 页面, then 一律被 `302/303` 重定向到 `/web/login`。
- Given 已登录会话已过 idle timeout 或 absolute lifetime, when operator 访问任意 `/web/*` 页面或提交 POST form, then 会话被视为失效、服务端不执行 mutation，并重定向回 `/web/login` 或给出等价显式失效反馈。
- Given 浏览器级 smoke test, when 执行核心流（login → dashboard → queue → detail → action）, then 流程可完成，且浏览器 console 中不存在 uncaught exception 或 error 级别日志。
- Given queue page filter, when operator 按 assignee/profile 过滤任务, then v1 统一映射到当前 assignee filter 语义，并展示对应任务列表。
- Given 浏览器禁用 JavaScript 或仅有极少量增强脚本, when 执行 login → dashboard → queue → detail → action 核心流, then 基本操作仍可通过 HTML form + POST-redirect-GET 完成。

## 10. Edge Cases / Failure Modes
- 登录失败
- session 过期
- API 409 conflict（reassign/retry 等）
- inactive assignee
- task 在打开 detail 后被其他 actor 修改
- scratchpad/messages 为空
- queue 无任务
- routing_metadata 不是合法 JSON（若采用 JSON 编辑）

## 11. Security / Privacy / Permission Notes
- Web session 不得把 admin JWT 直接暴露给浏览器端脚本、URL 或日志。
- login form 与已认证表单都必须具备服务端校验的 CSRF 防护；不得仅依赖 SameSite。
- 所有服务端调用 backend API 时必须沿用 admin 身份，并保持最小泄露面。
- UI 只暴露 admin 通过 API 已可访问的数据，不新增越权入口。
- 错误页/flash message 不得泄露 token、密码或内部堆栈中的敏感信息。

## 12. Rollout & Migration Notes
- Phase 3 在现有 FastAPI 服务内联扩展，不新增独立前端服务。
- Preflight go/no-go gate：在任何 Phase 3 编码开始前，必须先创建独立 Phase 3 feature branch/worktree，并确保此前 Phase 2 residue 已被隔离到清晰提交边界；未满足时不得进入实现。
- rollout 顺序建议：
  1. preflight：branch/worktree hygiene + artifact freeze
  2. 登录/session/CSRF 基础设施
  3. dashboard + queue 页面
  4. profiles 页面与 profile patch forms
  5. task detail 页面（含 degraded section handling）
  6. operator action forms
  7. server-side verification
  8. 浏览器级验证

## 13. Metrics / Success Signals
- 在验证环境中，operator 可通过 Web UI 完成 login → dashboard → queue → detail → action 的完整闭环，无需手写 API 请求。
- blocked / failed / stale 三类异常路径都至少有一条浏览器级 smoke test 覆盖并通过。
- UI 页面展示的 summary/workload 数值与对应 control-plane API 响应一致，不出现第二套聚合逻辑。
- 关键页面（login、dashboard、queue、detail）浏览器验证通过，且无 uncaught exception / error 级别 console log。

## 14. Deferred After v1 Freeze
- task detail 页面默认信息布局在 v1 中不作为 blocker；只要求信息可读、section 明确，视觉优先级优化留到后续迭代。
- dashboard 的 recent exceptions 列表不进入 v1 必交范围；v1 冻结为 summary cards + queue links 即可。

Design freeze summary:
server-rendered HTML + pure POST-redirect-GET; routing_metadata uses JSON textarea; queue filter maps to assignee_id contract; routing_metadata write semantics are replace-whole-object; web session uses server-side session store + bootstrap/login CSRF and authenticated session CSRF.

Related context: requirement contract and design/capability artifacts already aligned to this freeze.","toolsets":["skills"]}]}

{"results": [{"task_index": 0, "status": "completed", "summary": "STATUS: PASS
SUMMARY: The revised PRD is planning-ready: scope, reuse boundaries, security model, page-to-API mapping, rollout order, and acceptance criteria are specific enough to drive phased implementation without reopening product questions. The v1 freeze usefully locks the core architectural decisions (server-rendered HTML, POST-redirect-GET, admin-only session model, assignee_id filter semantics, replace-whole-object routing_metadata writes, API-first thin web layer), which reduces execution ambiguity and keeps sequencing clear across auth/session foundations, read surfaces, mutation forms, and browser verification.
PHASES:
- Preflight / branch hygiene: 1) create isolated Phase 3 feature branch/worktree, 2) verify Phase 2 residue is separated into clean commits, 3) freeze referenced PRD/design/capability artifacts before implementation starts.
- Web auth/session foundation: 1) add `/web/login` GET/POST, 2) implement server-side web session store and cookie policy, 3) add bootstrap CSRF for login and session-bound CSRF for authenticated POSTs, 4) implement logout/session expiry/rotation behavior, 5) add unauthenticated redirect handling for all `/web/*` routes.
- Shared web shell and dashboard/queues: 1) add minimal server-rendered layout/navigation/flash messaging, 2) implement dashboard by reusing existing overview summary contract, 3) implement blocked/failed/stale queue pages by reusing existing exception queue contract, 4) wire queue summary cards to queue pages, 5) freeze assignee/profile filter UI to existing `assignee_id` semantics.
- Profiles management surface: 1) implement profiles/workload page from existing registry/workload contract, 2) add profile patch forms for `is_active` and `routing_metadata`, 3) validate `routing_metadata` as JSON object before submit, 4) preserve replace-whole-object semantics, 5) surface success/failure feedback without partial writes.
- Task inspection and actions: 1) implement task detail page using existing task-scoped inspection surfaces, 2) render metadata/runs/events/messages/scratchpad with explicit empty states, 3) support partial-page degraded/error sections when one surface fails, 4) add reassign/unblock/retry/archive forms, 5) use POST-redirect-GET and existing mutation/state-machine contracts, 6) surface 409/inactive assignee/session-expiry failures explicitly.
- Verification and hardening: 1) add server-side tests for auth/session/CSRF/redirect/mutation failures, 2) add browser smoke tests for login → dashboard → queue → detail → action, 3) run with JS disabled/minimal JS to verify HTML form fallback, 4) confirm no dashboard/workload recomputation drift against backend APIs, 5) verify no uncaught console errors in core flows.
DEPENDENCIES:
- Phase 3 preflight must complete before any coding; branch/worktree isolation is an explicit go/no-go gate.
- Web auth/session work must precede all protected pages and all POST mutations.
- CSRF implementation must precede login submission hardening and all profile/task action forms.
- Shared layout/flash infrastructure should precede dashboard, queues, profiles, and task detail so success/error handling stays consistent.
- Dashboard and queue pages depend on stable Phase 2 overview and exception queue contracts being available and unchanged.
- Profiles page depends on existing profile registry/workload read contract and profile patch write contract.
- Task detail depends on all required task-scoped inspection surfaces being callable through existing access paths.
- Reassign/unblock/retry/archive UI depends on existing mutation endpoints/state-machine semantics and should not ship before error mapping for 409/auth/inactive assignee is defined in handlers.
- Browser smoke tests depend on the full login/session/CSRF flow plus at least one seeded blocked/failed/stale task and profile fixtures in the verification environment.
RISKS:
- Session/CSRF implementation drift from FastAPI defaults could create security regressions; mitigation: centralize middleware/helpers, test expiry/rotation/rejection paths explicitly, and verify cookie flags in responses.
- Thin web adapters may accidentally reintroduce business aggregation in the web layer; mitigation: enforce route-by-route contract mapping and reject any server-side recomputation beyond presentation shaping.
- Task detail combines multiple inspection surfaces, increasing risk of blank-page failures or inconsistent renders; mitigation: design per-section loading/error boundaries and test partial failure scenarios directly.
- `routing_metadata` textarea UX may produce malformed submissions or unintended overwrites; mitigation: validate JSON object shape before mutation, preserve replace-whole-object semantics, and echo clear validation errors with no partial update.
- Queue/filter terminology mismatch (`profile` vs `assignee`) could confuse operators and cause implementation drift; mitigation: freeze backend mapping to `assignee_id`, reflect this in labels/help text, and test filtered queue behavior end to end.
- No-JS/minimal-JS constraint can be eroded by convenience enhancements; mitigation: treat plain HTML form + redirect flow as the primary path and run browser validation with JavaScript disabled.
BLOCKING_FINDINGS:
- None.", "api_calls": 2, "duration_seconds": 45.54, "model": "gpt-5.4", "exit_reason": "completed", "tokens": {"input": 47494, "output": 1602}, "tool_trace": [{"tool": "skill_view", "args_bytes": 18, "result_bytes": 2101, "status": "ok"}]}, {"task_index": 1, "status": "completed", "summary": "STATUS: REQUEST_CHANGES
SUMMARY: The PRD is directionally strong and fits the existing Phase 1/2 architecture well: server-rendered HTML, POST-redirect-GET, admin-only scope, and strict reuse of control-plane contracts are all the right v1 choices. It is not yet implementation-safe, however, because three architecture-critical items remain under-specified: production-safe server-side session topology, bounded task-detail aggregation behavior, and the exact web-to-control-plane integration path.

KEY_FINDINGS:
- The overall architecture fits the current system well: embedding the console into the existing FastAPI service and freezing to server-rendered HTML + minimal JS minimizes drift, rollout risk, and duplicate business logic.
- The PRD does a good job preserving the Phase 2 contract boundary: dashboard, queues, profiles, and mutations are all explicitly tied to existing control-plane APIs, which is the right anti-drift constraint.
- Security posture is materially improved versus the earlier shape: opaque session cookie, dual CSRF model, PRG flow, and “no admin JWT in browser” are all strong decisions for an admin console.
- The session requirement is still incomplete at the architecture level: “server-side session store” is specified, but not whether it must be shared/durable across workers/instances; without that, timeout, logout invalidation, rotation, and POST safety can fail in production.
- Task detail aggregation is still underspecified for scale: runs/events/messages/scratchpad are required, but there is no frozen contract for ordering, per-section limits/pagination, or timeout budgets, which creates latency and page-weight risk on large tasks.
- The PRD allows a thin adapter/BFF helper, but does not freeze the approved integration pattern between web handlers and existing control-plane logic; without that, implementation may drift into loopback HTTP/token relay or duplicate orchestration logic.

TRADEOFFS:
- Server-rendered HTML + pure POST-redirect-GET / low complexity, easier verification, preserves API-first boundary, works without JS / less interactive UX and more full-page reloads / alternatives: HTMX-style progressive enhancement or SPA later.
- Server-side shared sessions / keeps secrets out of the browser, supports revocation and rotation, aligns with admin-console security needs / adds operational dependency and statefulness / alternatives: signed stateless cookies, but they are weaker for revocation and backend auth-context control.
- Bounded task-detail reads / protects latency, memory, and template rendering while enabling graceful degradation / operators may need extra clicks to view deeper history / alternatives: load-everything pages or richer JS progressive loading later.
- Thin adapter over existing control-plane contracts / avoids business-rule duplication while allowing page-shaped composition / adds one orchestration layer to maintain / alternatives: direct template calls into service layer or standalone frontend+BFF, both of which increase other forms of coupling.

BLOCKING_FINDINGS:
- Freeze that the Web session store must be shared and durable across FastAPI workers/instances; process-local/in-memory sessions are not acceptable for v1 because they break invalidation, rotation, and consistent auth behavior under real deployment topologies.
- Add an explicit bounded-read contract for `GET /web/tasks/{task_id}` sections: required ordering, per-section limit/pagination, and timeout budget/error isolation for runs, events, messages, and scratchpad. The current “show all inspection surfaces” requirement is not production-safe.
- Freeze the approved web-to-backend integration path so the console reuses existing control-plane logic without relaying raw admin JWT as the session source of truth or re-implementing orchestration rules. One explicit pattern must be mandated before coding.", "api_calls": 2, "duration_seconds": 56.39, "model": "gpt-5.4", "exit_reason": "completed", "tokens": {"input": 47478, "output": 2593}, "tool_trace": [{"tool": "skill_view", "args_bytes": 20, "result_bytes": 2001, "status": "ok"}]}], "total_duration_seconds": 58.2}

 ## 7.5. Reuse Contract / Page-to-API Mapping
+- Web route -> backend integration pattern 在 v1 中必须冻结为：`web route -> shared web service adapter -> existing hiveX control/task/auth helpers or thin backend-for-frontend adapter`。
+- 禁止把浏览器 session 直接等同于原始 admin JWT；admin JWT 只能作为服务端 session store 中受控持有的 backend auth context，由 web service adapter 在服务端内部使用。
+- 禁止在 Web route 中散落 loopback HTTP、自拼 token relay、重复 orchestration SQL/状态机；若需要页面级组合，只能通过共享 adapter 在服务端内部编排既有 contract。
 - `GET /web/login`：只负责渲染登录页，并 mint bootstrap CSRF token；不做业务聚合。
 - `POST /web/login`：复用现有 admin auth 语义建立 Web session；登录成功后跳转 dashboard。

-5. `POST /web/login` 必须使用 bootstrap CSRF；所有其他有副作用的 `POST /web/*` 都必须使用 authenticated session-bound CSRF；缺失或无效 token 时必须在 mutation 之前拒绝请求。

 - Given 一个可归档 task, when operator 在 queue 或 detail 页面执行 `archive`, then 页面反馈成功，刷新后该 task 状态为 `archived`，并且不再出现在 blocked/failed/stale queue 中。

{"results": [{"task_index": 0, "status": "completed", "summary": "STATUS: PASS
SUMMARY: The revised PRD is now planning-ready. It closes the prior planner blockers by freezing the three decisions that mattered most for safe decomposition: a shared durable server-side session store instead of client-visible token relay, bounded task-detail reads with stable ordering/default limits and section-level timeout isolation, and a single approved integration path of `web route -> shared web service adapter -> existing helpers or thin BFF adapter` with explicit prohibition on loopback HTTP token relay and business-rule reimplementation. Those choices align with Hermes’s existing shared SQLite/session architecture and reduce the main implementation-drift and security risks enough for design and task planning to proceed.

PHASES:
- Foundation and invariants
  - Treat the frozen session model, bounded-read semantics, and adapter-first integration pattern as non-negotiable implementation invariants.
  - Map each web route/action to the exact shared adapter entrypoint and then to existing helpers or a narrowly scoped BFF adapter where helper gaps exist.
- Session/auth implementation
  - Reuse the shared durable server-side session store for web auth/session continuity, including expiry, rotation/invalidation, and logout behavior.
  - Keep browser-visible state limited to session identifiers/cookies only; no loopback HTTP token relay.
- Task-detail read path
  - Implement bounded task-detail aggregation with explicit default limits, deterministic/stable ordering, and independent timeout/error isolation per section so slow subsections degrade without blocking the full page.
  - Define exact fallback behavior for partial data and timeout surfacing in the UI/API contract.
- Mutation/action path integration
  - Route state-changing web actions through the shared web service adapter into existing business logic/helpers.
  - Add only thin BFF adapters where data-shaping is required; do not fork business rules into web handlers.
- Verification and rollout
  - Add tests for session lifecycle, authz boundaries, bounded ordering/limits, timeout isolation, and adapter-vs-helper parity.
  - Validate that web routes and existing non-web surfaces produce consistent business outcomes for the same operations.

DEPENDENCIES:
- Existing durable shared session storage/session DB remains the source of truth for server-side session persistence and continuity.
- Existing helper/business-logic layer must be sufficiently reusable from the shared web service adapter; any gaps should be filled with thin adapters rather than route-local logic.
- Dashboard/web auth middleware and session enforcement must remain consistent across core and plugin-style routes.
- Task-detail data sources must support deterministic ordering and bounded pagination/limits at the query/helper layer, not only in the UI.

RISKS:
- Adapter drift risk: new web routes could still bypass the shared adapter unless route-to-helper bindings are made explicit in design and tests.
- Partial-render risk: section-level timeout isolation needs clear UX/API semantics so operators can distinguish degraded sections from missing data.
- Auth/session regression risk: server-side sessions reduce exposure, but invalidation/rotation/expiry behavior still needs concrete test coverage.
- Read amplification risk: task-detail aggregation can regress performance unless bounded limits are enforced at the source and not left to downstream filtering.

SUMMARY: The revised PRD closes the prior architecture blockers by freezing the three highest-risk seams: a shared durable server-side session store, bounded/stably ordered task-detail reads with default limits and section-level timeout isolation, and a single approved integration path through the shared web service adapter with existing helpers or a thin BFF adapter. That is sufficient for the PRD gate.
KEY_FINDINGS:
- The shared durable server-side session store removes the main consistency/scalability risk from per-instance or client-carried session state and is the right architectural default for multi-node web execution.
- Bounded task-detail reads with stable ordering and explicit default limits resolve the unbounded fan-out/read amplification concern and make pagination/perf behavior implementable and testable.
- Section-level timeout isolation is an adequate resilience boundary for partial task-detail rendering; one slow subsection no longer forces whole-page failure.
- Freezing `web route -> shared web service adapter -> existing helpers or thin BFF adapter` preserves layering and prevents architectural drift during implementation.
- Explicitly prohibiting loopback HTTP token relay and business-rule reimplementation removes the two biggest integration-regression paths: security leakage via token forwarding and divergence from existing domain logic.
TRADEOFFS:
- shared durable server-side session store / pros: consistency across instances, simpler failover semantics, safer auth/session lifecycle / cons: adds central dependency and operational statefulness / alternatives: stateless client sessions or per-instance memory, both rejected due to correctness and scale risks
- bounded stable-ordered reads with per-section timeout isolation / pros: predictable latency, safer large-task behavior, partial degradation instead of total failure / cons: some data may be deferred or omitted under limit/timeout pressure / alternatives: fully unbounded reads or single global timeout, both risk tail-latency and page-level instability
- shared adapter plus thin BFF only where needed / pros: reuses existing helpers, keeps business rules centralized, reduces duplication / cons: less short-term flexibility for bespoke route logic / alternatives: direct route-specific implementations or loopback proxying, both rejected because they increase drift and security complexity
